139: python scripts/tools/sync_resource_stubs.py
140: ```
141: 
142: This regenerates:
143: - `resources/resource_catalog.json`
144: - mapped `.tres` stubs under `resources/{characters,weapons,passives,accessories,enemies,evolutions,meta_upgrades,forge_recipes}/`
145: 
146: ## CI (GitHub Actions)
147: 
148: Workflow file: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
149: 
150: CI jobs:
151: - `validate-config-and-resources`: run JSON syntax gate (`scripts/tools/check_json_syntax.py`) before `scripts/validate_configs.py` and `scripts/check_resources.py`
152: - `godot-headless-and-gut`: run `godot --import`, headless startup check, GUT tests (unit + integration), quality baseline snapshot script (`scripts/tools/run_quality_baseline.gd`), release-gate rehearsal (`scripts/tools/run_release_gate.gd`), compatibility rehearsal (`scripts/tools/run_compatibility_rehearsal.gd`), resource-acceptance rehearsal with scene-visual checks (`scripts/tools/run_resource_acceptance.gd`), resource-acceptance trend+visual gate (second run), visual snapshot regression rehearsal (`scripts/tools/run_visual_snapshot_regression.gd` with `VISUAL_SNAPSHOT_BACKEND_TAG` + `VISUAL_SNAPSHOT_BACKEND_MATRIX` + `VISUAL_SNAPSHOT_STRATEGY=ci_rehearsal` + `VISUAL_SNAPSHOT_RUN_MODE=rehearsal` + `VISUAL_PIPELINE_ID`), visual snapshot trend gate (second run, `VISUAL_SNAPSHOT_STRATEGY=ci_trend_gate` + `VISUAL_SNAPSHOT_RUN_MODE=trend_gate` + `VISUAL_PIPELINE_ID`), and release template dry-runs (`VISUAL_SNAPSHOT_STRATEGY=release_candidate/release_blocking` + `VISUAL_PIPELINE_ID`) on `ubuntu-latest` + `windows-latest` matrix
153: 
154: Trigger:
155: - push (all branches)
156: - pull_request
157: 
158: Controls:
159: - `WASD` / Arrows: Move
160: - `Shift`: Sprint
161: - `E`: Next room (non-combat rooms / cleared combat rooms)
162: - Shop room: `1/2/3/4` buy slot, `R` restock
163: - Shop room: `T` exchange ore -> gold (limited trades per shop visit)
164: - Shop economy now shifts by chapter (`chapter_1`~`chapter_4`) with per-chapter price/restock/exchange tuning, quality-rate multipliers, and category bias
165: - Elite rooms are generated by run-plan metadata; they use elite-only combat waves and higher pressure
166: - Treasure rooms now run an elite mini-wave challenge before loot deployment; clear objective then collect rewards and press `E` to continue
167: - Route branching: after cleared rooms with multiple exits, use `1/2` to select route then `E` to confirm next room
168: - Route style now locks per chapter on first branch choice (`vanguard` / `raider`) and biases event weights, shop economy, hazard intensity, gold/ore drop tendency, route-tint visual feedback, and boss parameter profile (HP/Damage/Speed/Scale/Color)
169: - Level-up panel may show `Evolution: ...` option; selecting it upgrades current weapon profile according to `data/balance/evolutions.json`
170: - Safe camp: `F` damage forge (3 ore), `G` speed forge (5 ore)
171: - Safe camp: `T` open Memory Altar (in-run fragment archive)
172: - Safe camp route: `Q` holy route, `V` void route
173: - Chapter transition panel: `1/2` choose route, `E` confirm continue
174: - Narrative event panel: `1/2` choose event branch, `E` / `Esc` continue
175: - Chapter intro panel: `E` / `Esc` begin chapter
176: - Boss rooms: defeat 1 boss target to unlock doors and progress chapter
177: - Accessory drops: auto-equip up to 2 slots on pickup (extra converts to gold)
178: - Environment gauges: Frostbite/Void Corruption shown on HUD (builds in hazard rooms, decays in shop/camp)
179: - HUD Chapter Effects row: shows active timed effects with icon and remaining rooms (`[+]` boon / `[-]` curse / `[~]` mixed)
180: - HUD Route Style row: shows current style, per-chapter style comparison (`CH1~CH4`), and latest style-lock event
181: - HUD toast notifications for key milestones (achievement, ending, memory fragment, accessory, codex unlock)
182: - HUD minimap now prefers run-plan graph coordinates (`map_x/map_y` + `map_bounds`) for branch-aware layout, with index-grid fallback when coordinates are unavailable; unexplored rooms use `??`
183: - Accessories now include environment resistance bonuses (frost/void mitigation)
184: - `Esc`: Open/close Pause Panel during run
185: - Pause panel: `1` Resume, `2` Retreat (settle run), `3` Main Menu
186: - Pause panel settings subpage: `4` open settings, sliders apply instantly and persist
187: - Settings include Master/BGM/SFX/Ambience volume, Screen Shake strength, UI scale (0.8x-1.5x)
188: - Settings panel supports `Reset Defaults`, and changed values are highlighted in gold
189: - Settings panel includes audio bus meters (Master/BGM/SFX/Ambience) and `Play Test SFX` button
190: - Settings panel now supports input remap (keyboard + gamepad): click `Key`/`Pad` to listen, press target input to bind, `Esc` cancels capture
191: - Input remap has independent `Reset Input Defaults`; while listening, pause/menu close hotkeys are suppressed to prevent accidental panel close
192: - Run result panel: `E` / `Esc` to return menu
193: - Main menu memory archive: click `Review Next Memory Fragment` to cycle unlocked fragments
194: - Main menu ending archive: click `Review Next Ending` to cycle unlocked endings (story + first/repeat epilogue), with faction theme colors (Redeem/Fall/Balance)
195: - Run result panel now includes chapter-effect timeline replay for this run (room, chapter, effect icon, duration) with filter cycling (`ALL/BOON/CURSE/MIXED`, click or key `1`), mode tinting, in-session filter memory, view switch (`FLAT/GROUPED`, click or key `2`), grouped chapter-anchor navigation (`Q/E` or Anchor button), and grouped scope toggle (`ALL CHAPTERS` vs `ANCHOR ONLY`, click or key `3`)
196: - Run result panel now includes route-style evolution summary (chapter comparison + lock trajectory by room/chapter)
197: - Main menu now has a Settings overlay using the same runtime settings data as pause panel
198: - Main menu character switch: `Prev/Next` buttons, selection applies to next run and is saved
199: - Character trait is shown on selection panel and applied at run start before meta upgrades
200: - Main menu Codex: open with `Codex` button; use `Prev/Next` (or keys `1/2`) to browse categories, `Entry Prev/Next` (or keys `3/4`) to inspect entries, `Source/Chapter` filter buttons (or keys `5/6`) to filter statistics, `E/Esc` to close; detail pane shows status/hint/source/time/run index
201: - Codex statistics panel shows per-category completion bars, chapter completion bars (Chapter 1~4 + Global), global completion bar, and most recent 5 unlock records filtered by source/chapter (chapter comes from real unlock context metadata; filters persist across sessions)
202: 
203: Chapter transition blessing effects:
204: - `holy_vow`: +armor, +frost resistance, immediate heal
205: - `void_oath`: +weapon damage, +crit chance, -armor tradeoff
206: - Blessings are mutually exclusive: selecting a new blessing replaces the previous one
207: 
208: Chapter event lasting effects:
209: - Some event choices add a timed chapter effect for next 2-3 rooms
210: - Effects can modify enemy spawn rate/HP/damage, hazard intensity, move speed, damage bonus, and temporary armor delta
211: - Effects are shown in both room status and HUD Chapter Effects row, with remaining room count (`(nR)`)
212: 
213: Meta progression:
214: - Run ends on death / victory / retreat, shows result panel, then returns to menu
215: - Main menu shows meta currency, run stats, and last run reward
216: - Main menu also shows achievement/ending unlock progress and meta upgrades
217: - Main menu now lists unlocked memory fragments
218: - Save file path: `user://meta_save.json`
219: 
220: ## Validate data
221: 
222: ```bash
223: python scripts/tools/check_json_syntax.py
224: python scripts/validate_configs.py
225: python scripts/check_resources.py
226: ```
227: 
228: Validation now includes semantic checks (not only JSON syntax), including:
229: - fail-fast JSON syntax diagnostics (`scripts/tools/check_json_syntax.py`: file + line + column)
230: - enemy scaling floor coverage (1-15) and room-time bracket ordering
231: - enemy archetype weights, elite-wave interval sanity, and chapter archetype mapping integrity
232: - shop economy schema checks (discount, restock growth/cap, ore exchange rules, category weights)
233: - chapter-based shop overrides validation (`chapter_overrides` with bounded per-field ranges, quality/category multipliers, and pool overrides)
234: - route-style schema validation (`narrative_content.route_styles` including theme tint + boss profile fields, `chapter_events.weight_if_route_*`, `shop_items.route_style_overrides`)
235: - evolution schema validation (`evolutions.json`: ids, passive level gate, evolution_profile bounds)
236: - run-plan map generation schema validation (`map_generation.json`: chapter ranges, boss rooms, fixed rooms, weighted room typing)
237: - treasure challenge schema validation (`map_generation.json.treasure_challenge`: enable flag, combat mode, kill/reward scaling, accessory chances)
238: - run-plan output coordinate consistency validation (`map_bounds` span, per-room coordinate bounds, unique room coordinates)
239: - run-plan map generation now validates room-type weighting across base/chapter multipliers and per-chapter room-type caps (`max_room_type_per_chapter`) plus treasure cap compatibility
240: - enemy pooling regression resources are required and checked via `scripts/check_resources.py`
241: - boss phase threshold continuity rules (start at 1.0, strictly descending)
242: - drop table weight positivity and required tiers
243: - character config integrity (default id, unique ids, and stat ranges)
244: - character weapon profile integrity (mode/count/spread/jitter/hits/style)
245: - narrative event/transition structure and timed chapter-effect shape
246: - narrative index segment uniqueness and resource path verification
247: - quality baseline target schema (`quality_baseline_targets.json`: scenarios including boss endurance + frame-time/memory thresholds + compatibility matrix)
248: - quality baseline alert grading schema (`quality_baseline_targets.json.targets.alert_grading`: warning/critical ratios)
249: - release gate target schema (`release_gate_targets.json`: alert limits + required scenarios + compatibility/platform markers + publish blockers)
250: - compatibility rehearsal target schema (`compatibility_rehearsal_targets.json`: profiles + action bindings + release thresholds + publish blockers)
251: - resource acceptance target schema (`resource_acceptance_targets.json`: batch-6 production-ready IDs + acceptance_scene_matrix + scene_visual_requirements + acceptance_profile_policy + trend_baseline + ready-count/ready-ratio/smoke-depth/visual-check/profile-mismatch/trend-regression thresholds)
252: - visual snapshot regression schema (`visual_snapshot_targets.json`: chapter/ui snapshots + baseline_alignment (`required_snapshot_ids` + `allowed_capture_modes`) + precision convergence (`sample_rounds`/stddev) + backend profiles (`default/linux_headless/windows_headless`) + diff whitelist governance (`diff_whitelist` + `whitelist_policy`) + backend attribution (`backend_attribution`) + whitelist convergence (`whitelist_convergence`) + exception lifecycle policy (`exception_lifecycle`) + strategy orchestration (`strategy_orchestration`: default strategy + template overrides) + release gate templates (`release_gate_templates`: required strategy bindings + CI mode bindings + publish checklist) + backend matrix governance (`backend_matrix_governance`) + approval workflow (`approval_workflow`) + approval audit trail (`approval_audit_trail`) + approval history archive (`approval_history_archive`) + approval threshold templates (`approval_threshold_templates`) + release candidate tracking (`release_candidate_tracking`) + report_layers + cross_version_baseline + trend-drop gates)
253: - environment hazard animation schema (`environment_config.json chapter_*.hazard_anim_interval`: bounded animation cadence)
254: - environment ambient fx animation schema (`environment_config.json chapter_*.ambient_fx_interval`: bounded animation cadence)
255: - chapter visual profile schema (`environment_config.json chapter_*.visual_profile`: tint/alpha/scroll/pulse/wave ranges)
256: - tile atlas manifest schema (`assets/sprites/tiles/atlas_manifest.json`: style + atlas entries)
257: - resource catalog + stub schema (`resources/resource_catalog.json` + `resources/**/*.tres`: requires mapped `id/path/source_json/material_profile/asset_state/preview_texture/acceptance_scene/acceptance_profile/acceptance_tier` consistency, valid profile-policy tier mapping, valid source/material/preview/scene targets, scene-visual requirement count consistency, bucket-level counts consistency, and per-category ready-ratio consistency)
258: 
259: Smoke tests:
260: - `test/unit/test_character_weapon_profiles.gd` validates character weapon profile schema and projectile setup handshake
261: - `test/unit/test_enemy_scaling_profiles.gd` validates enemy archetype weights, elite-wave interval schema, and chapter archetype mapping keys
262: - `test/unit/test_shop_economy_config.gd` validates dynamic shop economy tuning fields, chapter quality/category overrides, and progression consistency
263: - `test/unit/test_narrative_route_style_config.gd` validates route-style profiles (including theme/boss fields) and chapter-event route weight keys
264: - `test/unit/test_map_generation_config.gd` validates map-generation schema and generated run-plan invariants
265: - `test/unit/test_map_generation_config.gd` also validates treasure-room schema keys and generated treasure room types
266: - `test/unit/test_evolutions_config.gd` validates evolution recipe schema and evolution_profile bounds
267: - `test/unit/test_enemy_pooling.gd` validates enemy pool lifecycle reset and death-release behavior
268: - `test/unit/test_quality_baseline_targets.gd` validates quality baseline scenario/threshold schema (including boss endurance setup) and required InputMap actions
269: - `test/unit/test_release_gate_targets.gd` validates release gate schema, required scenario list, required input-action bindings, and CI platform markers
270: - `test/unit/test_compatibility_rehearsal_targets.gd` validates compatibility rehearsal schema, profile markers, required actions, and CI platform markers
271: - `test/unit/test_resource_acceptance_targets.gd` validates resource acceptance target schema, required production-ready IDs, acceptance scene matrix, scene_visual_requirements, acceptance-profile policy mapping, trend baseline thresholds, and per-category ready-count/ready-ratio plus bucket-ratio guards
272: - `test/unit/test_resource_directory_baseline.gd` validates resource catalog v8 structure, mapped stub/source/material/preview/acceptance consistency, scene-visual requirement counts, acceptance-tier semantics, production-ready/tier/bucket counts, per-category ready-ratio consistency, and balance-ID coverage across resource families
273: - `test/unit/test_visual_snapshot_targets.gd` validates visual snapshot target schema, baseline-alignment references, diff whitelist/whitelist-policy bounds, backend-attribution/whitelist-convergence/exception-lifecycle policy bounds, strategy-orchestration structure/references/override-shape, release-gate-template manifest bindings/checklist constraints, backend-matrix governance + approval-workflow + approval-audit-trail + approval-history-archive constraints, approval-threshold-template and release-candidate-tracking constraints, precision/backend-profile bounds, report layer constraints, cross-version baseline references, tightened trend thresholds, and referenced snapshot scenes existence
274: - `test/unit/test_visual_tilemap_layers.gd` validates game_world TileMap visual layers, chapter-specific resource tileset switching (including ground-detail + ambient-fx layers), hazard overlay state reaction, hazard/ambient atlas animation ticking, chapter visual-profile tint/scroll plus pulse/wave feedback, and handdrawn atlas manifest guardrails
275: - `test/integration/test_core_flow_regression.gd` validates character-start->progress->settlement persistence, level-up evolution path, baseline/new-cycle shop purchase closure (including D13 + D18 weapon branches), and stat feedback

(End of file - total 275 lines)
</content>

107: 
108: ### 阶段 D：表现层升级（优先级 P2）
109: 
110: - 主场景 TileMap 化（地表 -> 门区块 -> 危害层逐步替换）
111: - 保持系统接口不变，降低行为回归风险
112: - 验收标准
113:   - 玩法行为一致。
114:   - 视觉层替换不影响既有测试通过率。
115:

2: 
3: ## 核对范围
4: 
5: - 核对文档：`requirements.md`、`architecture.md`、`core-systems.md`、`data-structures.md`、`level-design.md`、`test-plan.md`、`roadmap.md`、`supplement-updates.md`

7: - 核对口径：按**全量文档承诺**评估，不仅限 MVP
8: 
9: ## 总体结论
10: 
11: - 核心玩法主干已成型：战斗、刷怪压力、Run-Plan、房间推进、叙事权重、结算与存档链路均可在代码中确认。
12: - 主要缺口集中在内容规模与资产结构：角色/进化/商店池深度偏小，资源目录与场景体量尚未达到文档全量形态。
13: - 集成回归能力已起步：关键流程（开局推进结算、进化、商店闭环）已有场景级测试护栏。
14: - 当前仓库状态更接近“可玩 MVP + 扩展基础设施”，与文档中的完整版目标存在阶段性差距。
15: 
16: ## 真值矩阵
17:

21: | EventBus 类型化信号总线 | 已确认 | `scripts/autoload/event_bus.gd` | 运行状态、战斗、房间、路线、UI、设置等信号均有实现。 |
22: | 配置中心统一加载 balance JSON | 已确认 | `scripts/autoload/config_manager.gd`, `data/balance/*.json` | `CONFIG_PATHS` 全量加载关键配置。 |
23: | 核心战斗与敌人压力模型 | 已确认 | `scripts/systems/damage_system.gd`, `scripts/systems/enemy_spawner.gd`, `scripts/game/enemy.gd` | 包含护甲上限、圣光穿透、房间时间+楼层缩放、精英与 Boss 逻辑。 |
24: | Run-Plan 地图生成与分支 | 已确认 | `scripts/systems/map_generator.gd`, `scripts/game/game_world.gd` | 房间类型、章节归属、节点连接、路线推进均可追踪。 |
25: | 宝藏房挑战模式（精英小波次后发奖） | 已确认 | `data/balance/map_generation.json`, `scripts/game/game_world.gd`, `scripts/validate_configs.py`, `test/unit/test_map_generation_config.gd` | 已支持可配置挑战参数（击杀门槛/奖励缩放/饰品概率）并具备配置与测试护栏。 |
26: | 叙事权重、路线风格、结局门槛 | 已确认 | `scripts/systems/narrative_system.gd`, `data/balance/narrative_content.json` | 包含 alignment、route style、recent 抑制、ending gate。 |
27: | HUD / 暂停 / 运行时设置 / 结算面板 | 已确认 | `scripts/ui/hud.gd`, `scripts/ui/pause_panel.gd`, `scripts/ui/runtime_settings_panel.gd`, `scripts/ui/run_result_panel.gd` | 信息反馈与设置持久化链路完整。 |
28: | 进化系统深度 | 部分实现 | `scripts/systems/level_up_system.gd`, `scripts/game/auto_weapon.gd`, `data/balance/evolutions.json` | 机制就绪且已扩容到 13 条 recipe，并可在升级链路中消费，但距离全量规格仍有差距。 |
29: | 叙事记忆碎片体系 | 部分实现 | `data/balance/narrative_index.json`, `data/balance/narrative_content.json` | 索引与系统已接通，碎片内容已扩容到 22 条，但与全量叙事目标相比仍有提升空间。 |
30: | 角色/武器/被动/商店内容深度 | 部分实现 | `data/balance/characters.json`, `data/balance/shop_items.json`, `scripts/game/game_world.gd`, `test/unit/test_content_depth_targets.gd`, `test/integration/test_core_flow_regression.gd` | 已扩容至角色 12、商店 passive 14 / weapon 15，且新增条目已有消费逻辑与阈值+集成护栏，仍需继续向全量目标推进。 |
31: | 大规模资源目录（以 `.tres` 为主） | 部分实现 | `resources/`, `resources/resource_catalog.json`, `resources/{characters,weapons,passives,accessories,enemies,evolutions,meta_upgrades,forge_recipes}`, `resources/material_profiles/*.tres`, `scripts/tools/sync_resource_stubs.py`, `scripts/tools/run_resource_acceptance.gd`, `data/balance/resource_acceptance_targets.json`, `scripts/check_resources.py`, `test/unit/test_resource_directory_baseline.gd`, `test/unit/test_resource_acceptance_targets.gd` | 已补齐 8 类资源目录并从“样例 stub”扩展到“按内容 ID 批量映射 + 材质参数资产化 + production_ready 分层验收（catalog v8/batch_6）”，当前验收已覆盖“按类 ready 数量/比例 + 跨场景 smoke 深度 + acceptance_profile 分层语义一致性 + 分层趋势基线（bucket ratio + ratio drop）+ 场景内可视节点/属性检查（含 bool_true）+ production_ready_ratio_by_category 一致性”护栏；距离全量生产级实料内容仍有差距。 |
32: | 主玩法场景 TileMap 化呈现 | 已确认（阶段 D） | `scenes/game/game_world.tscn`, `scripts/game/game_world.gd`, `assets/sprites/tiles/*.png`, `assets/sprites/tiles/atlas_manifest.json`, `resources/tilesets/*.tres`, `data/balance/environment_config.json`, `test/unit/test_visual_tilemap_layers.gd` | 已完成地表/地表细节/门区块/危害/环境特效五层 TileMap 化，并从运行时程序化 tileset 切换为资源化 tileset；门/危害/环境特效层支持章节差异化 tileset 动态切换，危害与特效具备章节化动画节奏驱动，`visual_profile` 已支持细节层 tint/alpha+pulse 与特效层滚动+wave 参数化控制，且 handdrawn atlas 管线与 manifest 护栏已落地。 |
33: | 测试计划中的集成/性能/兼容矩阵 | 部分实现 | `test/unit/*.gd`, `test/integration/test_core_flow_regression.gd`, `data/balance/quality_baseline_targets.json`, `data/balance/release_gate_targets.json`, `data/balance/compatibility_rehearsal_targets.json`, `data/balance/visual_snapshot_targets.json`, `scripts/tools/check_json_syntax.py`, `scripts/tools/run_quality_baseline.gd`, `scripts/tools/run_release_gate.gd`, `scripts/tools/run_compatibility_rehearsal.gd`, `scripts/tools/run_visual_snapshot_regression.gd`, `test/unit/test_quality_baseline_targets.gd`, `test/unit/test_release_gate_targets.gd`, `test/unit/test_compatibility_rehearsal_targets.gd`, `test/unit/test_visual_snapshot_targets.gd`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `spec-design/test-plan.md` | 已补关键流程集成回归；性能基线已扩展为 `elite_pressure_medium/high/extreme + boss_pressure_endurance` 分层采样并支持告警分级；已新增发布门演练、profile 级兼容演练，以及章节级可视快照指标回归（D36：`chapter_snapshot_v12` + `strategy_orchestration` 策略模板编排 + `release_gate_templates` 发布清单/门禁模板固化 + `backend_matrix_governance` 跨后端矩阵治理 + `approval_workflow` 审批流程硬约束 + `approval_audit_trail` 审批历史持久化与跨流水线追溯 + `approval_history_archive` 长期归档聚合与跨后端趋势 delta 约束 + `approval_threshold_templates` 分层审批阈值模板 + `release_candidate_tracking` 发布候选追踪评估 + `report_layers` 分层收敛 + `cross_version_baseline` 跨版本漂移约束 + `backend_attribution` 归因阻断 + `whitelist_convergence` 收敛建议 + `exception_lifecycle` 生命周期治理 + 趋势 gate）；并在 D30-1 前置 JSON 语法快速失败闸门（file:line:column 定位）。CI 已覆盖 ubuntu+windows 双平台矩阵，但大规模性能压测与跨平台实机矩阵仍未完全落地。 |
34: 
35: ## 风险分级
36: 
37: - 高：文档全量承诺与仓库现状差距若不显式标注，会持续放大协作预期偏差。
38: - 中：内容深度不足会影响长线可玩性与构筑差异化体验。
39: - 中：已补关键流程集成回归并启动质量基线采样，但性能压测深度与跨平台实机覆盖仍薄弱。
40: - 低：表现层主链路已完成资源化接入，后续主要是临时 atlas 的手绘替换与细节打磨。
41: - 低：当前系统主干稳定，具备持续增量扩展基础。
42: 
43: ## 建议推进顺序
44: 
45: 1. 文档对齐（先消除预期偏差）
46: 2. 内容扩容（角色/进化/商店池）
47: 3. 表现升级（TileMap 与场景视觉管线）
48: 4. 测试补强（集成回归 + 性能基线）
49:

**Output:**
```
<path>D:\Desktop\Project\Game\angel-fallen\spec-design\README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # 幸存者Roguelike - OpenSpec 项目规格文档
2: 
3: ## 项目概述
4: 
5: **项目名称**: Survivor Roguelike  
6: **类型**: 动作 Roguelike / 类吸血鬼幸存者  
7: **引擎**: Godot 4.x + GDScript  
8: **平台**: PC 桌面端 (Windows / Linux / macOS)  
9: **视角**: 俯视角 2D  
10: 
11: ## 核心理念
12: 
13: 将经典幸存者玩法（自动攻击、海量怪物、升级选技能）与 Roguelike 元素（随机地图生成、地牢探索、永久死亡、Meta 进度）深度融合，打造一款具有高重玩性的动作生存游戏。
14: 
15: ## 文档索引
16: 
17: | 文档 | 说明 |
18: |------|------|
19: | [requirements.md](./requirements.md) | 功能需求与非功能需求规格 |
20: | [architecture.md](./architecture.md) | 系统架构与模块划分 |
21: | [core-systems.md](./core-systems.md) | 核心系统详细设计 |
22: | [data-structures.md](./data-structures.md) | 数据结构、资源定义与配置表 |
23: | [test-plan.md](./test-plan.md) | 测试方案与质量保证 |
24: | [roadmap.md](./roadmap.md) | 实施路线图与里程碑 |

10: func _initialize() -> void:

321: 
322: 	DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(SNAPSHOT_DIR)
323: 	var allowed_capture_modes: Array[String] = _resolve_allowed_capture_modes(allowed_capture_modes_map, backend_tag)
324: 
325: 	var passed_snapshots: int = 0
326: 	for snapshot_id_var in snapshots.keys():
327: 		var snapshot_id: String = str(snapshot_id_var)
328: 		var row_variant: Variant = snapshots.get(snapshot_id, {})
329: 		if not (row_variant is Dictionary):
330: 			_push_result(report, snapshot_id, "invalid_snapshot_row", false, "snapshot row must be object")
331: 			continue
332: 
333: 		var row: Dictionary = row_variant
334: 		var capture_result: Dictionary = await _capture_snapshot(snapshot_id, row, sample_rounds)
335: 		if not bool(capture_result.get("ok", false)):
336: 			_push_result(report, snapshot_id, "capture_failed", false, str(capture_result.get("message", "capture failed")))
337: 			continue
338: 
339: 		var metrics: Dictionary = capture_result.get("metrics", {})
340: 		var snapshot_metrics: Dictionary = report.get("snapshots", {})
341: 		snapshot_metrics[snapshot_id] = metrics
342: 		report["snapshots"] = snapshot_metrics
343: 
344: 		var opaque_ratio: float = float(metrics.get("opaque_ratio", 0.0))
345: 		var unique_colors: int = int(metrics.get("unique_colors", 0))
346: 		var avg_luma: float = float(metrics.get("avg_luma", 0.0))
347: 		var capture_mode: String = str(metrics.get("capture_mode", "unknown"))
348: 
349: 		var opaque_ratio_min: float = float(row.get("opaque_ratio_min", 0.0))
350: 		var unique_colors_min: int = int(ceil(float(row.get("unique_colors_min", 0)) * unique_colors_min_scale))
351: 		var luma_min: float = clampf(float(row.get("luma_min", 0.0)) - luma_range_padding, 0.0, 1.0)
352: 		var luma_max: float = clampf(float(row.get("luma_max", 1.0)) + luma_range_padding, 0.0, 1.0)
353: 
354: 		var snapshot_passed: bool = true
355: 		if opaque_ratio < opaque_ratio_min:
356: 			snapshot_passed = false
357: 			_push_result(report, snapshot_id, "opaque_ratio_min", false, "opaque_ratio %.3f below min %.3f" % [opaque_ratio, opaque_ratio_min])
358: 		if unique_colors < unique_colors_min:
359: 			snapshot_passed = false
360: 			_push_result(report, snapshot_id, "unique_colors_min", false, "unique_colors %d below min %d" % [unique_colors, unique_colors_min])
361: 		if avg_luma < luma_min or avg_luma > luma_max:
362: 			snapshot_passed = false
363: 			_push_result(report, snapshot_id, "luma_range", false, "avg_luma %.3f out of range [%.3f, %.3f]" % [avg_luma, luma_min, luma_max])
364: 		if not allowed_capture_modes.is_empty() and not allowed_capture_modes.has(capture_mode):
365: 			snapshot_passed = false
366: 			_push_result(report, snapshot_id, "capture_mode_not_allowed", false, "capture_mode %s not allowed for backend %s" % [capture_mode, backend_tag])
367: 
368: 		var opaque_ratio_stddev: float = float(metrics.get("opaque_ratio_stddev", 0.0))
369: 		var luma_stddev: float = float(metrics.get("avg_luma_stddev", 0.0))
370: 		var unique_stddev_ratio: float = float(metrics.get("unique_color_stddev_ratio", 0.0))
371: 		if opaque_ratio_stddev > max_opaque_ratio_stddev:
372: 			snapshot_passed = false
373: 			_push_result(report, snapshot_id, "opaque_ratio_stddev", false, "opaque_ratio_stddev %.4f exceeds %.4f" % [opaque_ratio_stddev, max_opaque_ratio_stddev])
374: 		if luma_stddev > max_luma_stddev:
375: 			snapshot_passed = false
376: 			_push_result(report, snapshot_id, "luma_stddev", false, "avg_luma_stddev %.4f exceeds %.4f" % [luma_stddev, max_luma_stddev])
377: 		if unique_stddev_ratio > max_unique_color_stddev_ratio:
378: 			snapshot_passed = false
379: 			_push_result(report, snapshot_id, "unique_color_stddev_ratio", false, "unique_color_stddev_ratio %.4f exceeds %.4f" % [unique_stddev_ratio, max_unique_color_stddev_ratio])
380: 
381: 		var previous_snapshots: Dictionary = previous_report.get("snapshots", {})
382: 		var previous_metrics: Dictionary = previous_snapshots.get(snapshot_id, {})
383: 		if not previous_metrics.is_empty():
384: 			var prev_opaque_ratio: float = float(previous_metrics.get("opaque_ratio", opaque_ratio))
385: 			var prev_unique_colors: float = float(previous_metrics.get("unique_colors", unique_colors))
386: 			var prev_luma: float = float(previous_metrics.get("avg_luma", avg_luma))
387: 
388: 			var opaque_drop: float = maxf(0.0, prev_opaque_ratio - opaque_ratio)
389: 			var unique_drop_ratio: float = 0.0
390: 			if prev_unique_colors > 0.0:
391: 				unique_drop_ratio = maxf(0.0, (prev_unique_colors - float(unique_colors)) / prev_unique_colors)
392: 			var luma_delta: float = absf(prev_luma - avg_luma)
393: 			var whitelist_row: Dictionary = _resolve_snapshot_whitelist(diff_whitelist, backend_tag, snapshot_id)
394: 			var whitelist_reason: String = str(whitelist_row.get("reason", ""))
395: 			var effective_opaque_drop: float = max_opaque_ratio_drop
396: 			var effective_unique_drop: float = max_unique_color_drop_ratio
397: 			var effective_luma_delta: float = max_luma_delta
398: 			if whitelist_row.has("max_opaque_ratio_drop"):
399: 				effective_opaque_drop = maxf(effective_opaque_drop, float(whitelist_row.get("max_opaque_ratio_drop", effective_opaque_drop)))
400: 			if whitelist_row.has("max_unique_color_drop_ratio"):
401: 				effective_unique_drop = maxf(effective_unique_drop, float(whitelist_row.get("max_unique_color_drop_ratio", effective_unique_drop)))
402: 			if whitelist_row.has("max_luma_delta"):
403: 				effective_luma_delta = maxf(effective_luma_delta, float(whitelist_row.get("max_luma_delta", effective_luma_delta)))
404: 
405: 			var trend: Dictionary = report.get("trend", {})
406: 			var opaque_ratio_drops: Dictionary = trend.get("opaque_ratio_drops", {})
407: 			opaque_ratio_drops[snapshot_id] = opaque_drop
408: 			trend["opaque_ratio_drops"] = opaque_ratio_drops
409: 			var unique_color_drop_ratios: Dictionary = trend.get("unique_color_drop_ratios", {})
410: 			unique_color_drop_ratios[snapshot_id] = unique_drop_ratio
411: 			trend["unique_color_drop_ratios"] = unique_color_drop_ratios
412: 			var luma_deltas: Dictionary = trend.get("luma_deltas", {})
413: 			luma_deltas[snapshot_id] = luma_delta
414: 			trend["luma_deltas"] = luma_deltas
415: 			report["trend"] = trend
416: 
417: 			if opaque_drop > max_opaque_ratio_drop:
418: 				if opaque_drop <= effective_opaque_drop:
419: 					_register_whitelist_hit(report, snapshot_id, "opaque_ratio_drop", opaque_drop, max_opaque_ratio_drop, effective_opaque_drop, whitelist_reason)
420: 				else:
421: 					_push_warning(report, snapshot_id, "opaque_ratio_drop", "opaque_ratio drop %.3f exceeds %.3f" % [opaque_drop, effective_opaque_drop])
422: 					trend["regressions"] = int(trend.get("regressions", 0)) + 1
423: 					report["trend"] = trend
424: 			if unique_drop_ratio > max_unique_color_drop_ratio:
425: 				if unique_drop_ratio <= effective_unique_drop:
426: 					_register_whitelist_hit(report, snapshot_id, "unique_color_drop_ratio", unique_drop_ratio, max_unique_color_drop_ratio, effective_unique_drop, whitelist_reason)
427: 				else:
428: 					_push_warning(report, snapshot_id, "unique_color_drop_ratio", "unique_colors drop ratio %.3f exceeds %.3f" % [unique_drop_ratio, effective_unique_drop])
429: 					trend["regressions"] = int(trend.get("regressions", 0)) + 1
430: 					report["trend"] = trend
431: 			if luma_delta > max_luma_delta:
432: 				if luma_delta <= effective_luma_delta:
433: 					_register_whitelist_hit(report, snapshot_id, "luma_delta", luma_delta, max_luma_delta, effective_luma_delta, whitelist_reason)
434: 				else:
435: 					_push_warning(report, snapshot_id, "luma_delta", "luma delta %.3f exceeds %.3f" % [luma_delta, effective_luma_delta])
436: 					trend["regressions"] = int(trend.get("regressions", 0)) + 1
437: 					report["trend"] = trend
438: 
439: 		if snapshot_passed:
440: 			passed_snapshots += 1
441: 			_push_result(report, snapshot_id, "snapshot_metrics", true, "snapshot metrics within baseline")
442: 
443: 	if passed_snapshots < min_snapshots:
444: 		_push_result(report, "global", "min_snapshots", false, "passed snapshots %d below min_snapshots %d" % [passed_snapshots, min_snapshots])
445: 
446: 	for required_id in required_snapshot_ids:
447: 		var captured_snapshots: Dictionary = report.get("snapshots", {})
448: 		if not captured_snapshots.has(required_id):
449: 			_push_result(report, "global", "required_snapshot_not_captured", false, "required snapshot '%s' missing from report" % required_id)
450: 
451: 	var trend_row: Dictionary = report.get("trend", {})
452: 	var trend_regressions: int = int(trend_row.get("regressions", 0))
453: 	if trend_regressions > max_trend_regressions:
454: 		_push_result(report, "global", "max_trend_regressions_exceeded", false, "trend regressions %d exceeds %d" % [trend_regressions, max_trend_regressions])
455: 
456: 	var whitelist_row: Dictionary = report.get("whitelist", {})
457: 	var whitelist_hits: int = int(whitelist_row.get("hits", 0))
458: 	var whitelist_ratio: float = 0.0
459: 	if snapshots.size() > 0:
460: 		whitelist_ratio = float(whitelist_hits) / float(snapshots.size())
461: 	whitelist_row["ratio"] = whitelist_ratio
462: 	report["whitelist"] = whitelist_row
463: 	if whitelist_hits > whitelist_max_hits:
464: 		_push_result(report, "global", "whitelist_max_hits_exceeded", false, "whitelist hits %d exceeds %d" % [whitelist_hits, whitelist_max_hits])
465: 	if whitelist_ratio > whitelist_max_ratio:
466: 		_push_result(report, "global", "whitelist_max_ratio_exceeded", false, "whitelist ratio %.3f exceeds %.3f" % [whitelist_ratio, whitelist_max_ratio])
467: 
468: 	_evaluate_backend_attribution(report, backend_tag, required_backends, max_unattributed_regressions, max_backend_specific_regressions)
469: 	_evaluate_whitelist_convergence(report, previous_report, stale_run_threshold, tighten_margin_ratio, max_suggestions)
470: 	_evaluate_exception_lifecycle(report, previous_report, expire_idle_runs, auto_reclaim_hit_streak, max_expired_entries, max_reclaim_candidates)
471: 
472: 	_evaluate_cross_version_baseline(report, cross_version_baseline)
473: 	_evaluate_report_layers(report, report_layers, snapshots)
474: 	_evaluate_release_gate_templates(
475: 		report,
476: 		release_gate_templates,
477: 		targets.get("strategy_orchestration", {}),
478: 		strategy_name,
479: 		strategy_template,
480: 		run_mode
481: 	)
482: 	_evaluate_backend_matrix_governance(report, backend_matrix_governance, release_gate_templates, run_mode)
483: 	_evaluate_approval_workflow(report, approval_workflow, strategy_name)
484: 	_evaluate_approval_audit_trail(report, approval_audit_trail, strategy_name, run_mode, backend_tag)
485: 	_evaluate_approval_history_archive(report, approval_history_archive, strategy_name, run_mode, backend_tag)
486: 	_evaluate_release_candidate_tracking(report, release_candidate_tracking, run_mode)
487: 	_evaluate_release_candidate_score(report, release_candidate_scoring, run_mode)
488: 	_evaluate_approval_convergence_dashboard(report, approval_convergence_dashboard, run_mode)
489: 
490: 	var failures: int = int(report.get("blockers", 0))
491: 	if failures > max_failures:
492: 		_push_result(report, "global", "max_failures_exceeded", false, "blockers %d exceeds max_failures %d" % [failures, max_failures])
493: 
494: 	return report
495: 
496: 
497: func _register_whitelist_hit(
498: 	report: Dictionary,
499: 	snapshot_id: String,
500: 	rule_id: String,
501: 	actual: float,
502: 	default_limit: float,
503: 	effective_limit: float,
504: 	reason: String
505: ) -> void:
506: 	var whitelist_row: Dictionary = report.get("whitelist", {})
507: 	var items: Array = whitelist_row.get("items", [])
508: 	items.append(
509: 		{
510: 			"snapshot": snapshot_id,
511: 			"id": rule_id,
512: 			"actual": actual,
513: 			"default_limit": default_limit,
514: 			"effective_limit": effective_limit,
515: 			"reason": reason,
516: 		}
517: 	)
518: 	whitelist_row["items"] = items
519: 	whitelist_row["hits"] = int(whitelist_row.get("hits", 0)) + 1
520: 	report["whitelist"] = whitelist_row
521: 	var message: String = "%s %.3f exceeds default %.3f but allowed by whitelist %.3f" % [rule_id, actual, default_limit, effective_limit]
522: 	if not reason.is_empty():
523: 		message += " (%s)" % reason
524: 	_push_warning(report, snapshot_id, "%s_whitelisted" % rule_id, message)
525: 
526: 
527: func _evaluate_backend_attribution(
528: 	report: Dictionary,
529: 	backend_tag: String,
530: 	required_backends: Array[String],
531: 	max_unattributed_regressions: int,
532: 	max_backend_specific_regressions: int
533: ) -> void:
534: 	var attribution_row: Dictionary = report.get("backend_attribution", {})
535: 	var snapshot_metrics: Dictionary = report.get("snapshots", {})
536: 	var items: Array = report.get("items", [])
537: 
538: 	var regressions: int = 0
539: 	var backend_specific_regressions: int = 0
540: 	var unattributed_regressions: int = 0
541: 	var attribution_items: Array = []
542: 
543: 	for item in items:
544: 		if not (item is Dictionary):
545: 			continue
546: 		var row: Dictionary = item
547: 		if bool(row.get("passed", false)):
548: 			continue
549: 		if str(row.get("severity", "blocker")) != "warning":
550: 			continue
551: 		var rule_id: String = str(row.get("id", ""))
552: 		if rule_id != "opaque_ratio_drop" and rule_id != "unique_color_drop_ratio" and rule_id != "luma_delta":
553: 			continue
554: 
555: 		regressions += 1
556: 		var snapshot_id: String = str(row.get("snapshot", ""))
557: 		var capture_mode: String = "unknown"
558: 		if snapshot_metrics.has(snapshot_id):
559: 			var metric_row: Variant = snapshot_metrics.get(snapshot_id, {})
560: 			if metric_row is Dictionary:
561: 				capture_mode = str(metric_row.get("capture_mode", "unknown"))
562: 
563: 		var attribution: String = "unattributed"
564: 		if capture_mode.begins_with("fallback_"):
565: 			attribution = backend_tag
566: 			backend_specific_regressions += 1
567: 		else:
568: 			unattributed_regressions += 1
569: 
570: 		attribution_items.append({
571: 			"snapshot": snapshot_id,
572: 			"id": rule_id,
573: 			"capture_mode": capture_mode,
574: 			"attribution": attribution,
575: 		})
576: 
577: 	attribution_row["backend_tag"] = backend_tag
578: 	attribution_row["required_backends"] = required_backends
579: 	attribution_row["regressions"] = regressions
580: 	attribution_row["backend_specific_regressions"] = backend_specific_regressions
581: 	attribution_row["max_backend_specific_regressions"] = max_backend_specific_regressions
582: 	attribution_row["unattributed_regressions"] = unattributed_regressions
583: 	attribution_row["max_unattributed_regressions"] = max_unattributed_regressions
584: 	attribution_row["items"] = attribution_items
585: 	report["backend_attribution"] = attribution_row
586: 
587: 	if not required_backends.is_empty() and not required_backends.has(backend_tag):
588: 		_push_result(report, "global", "backend_not_allowed", false, "backend %s is not listed in backend_attribution.required_backends" % backend_tag)
589: 
590: 	if unattributed_regressions > max_unattributed_regressions:
591: 		_push_result(report, "global", "max_unattributed_regressions_exceeded", false, "unattributed regressions %d exceeds %d" % [unattributed_regressions, max_unattributed_regressions])
592: 
593: 	if backend_specific_regressions > max_backend_specific_regressions:
594: 		_push_result(report, "global", "max_backend_specific_regressions_exceeded", false, "%s regressions %d exceeds %d" % [backend_tag, backend_specific_regressions, max_backend_specific_regressions])
595: 
596: 
597: func _evaluate_whitelist_convergence(
598: 	report: Dictionary,
599: 	previous_report: Dictionary,
600: 	stale_run_threshold: int,
601: 	tighten_margin_ratio: float,
602: 	max_suggestions: int
603: ) -> void:
604: 	var whitelist_row: Dictionary = report.get("whitelist", {})
605: 	var whitelist_items: Array = whitelist_row.get("items", [])
606: 	var previous_whitelist: Dictionary = previous_report.get("whitelist", {})
607: 	var previous_streaks: Dictionary = previous_whitelist.get("streaks", {})
608: 
609: 	var streaks: Dictionary = {}
610: 	var suggestions: Array = []
611: 
612: 	for item in whitelist_items:
613: 		if not (item is Dictionary):
614: 			continue
615: 		var row: Dictionary = item
616: 		var snapshot_id: String = str(row.get("snapshot", ""))
617: 		var rule_id: String = str(row.get("id", ""))
618: 		if snapshot_id.is_empty() or rule_id.is_empty():
619: 			continue
620: 
621: 		var key: String = "%s|%s" % [snapshot_id, rule_id]
622: 		var previous_streak: int = int(previous_streaks.get(key, 0))
623: 		var current_streak: int = previous_streak + 1
624: 		streaks[key] = current_streak
625: 
626: 		if current_streak < stale_run_threshold:
627: 			continue
628: 		if max_suggestions > 0 and suggestions.size() >= max_suggestions:
629: 			continue
630: 
631: 		var default_limit: float = float(row.get("default_limit", 0.0))
632: 		var effective_limit: float = float(row.get("effective_limit", default_limit))
633: 		if effective_limit <= default_limit:
634: 			continue
635: 
636: 		var suggested_limit: float = default_limit + (effective_limit - default_limit) * (1.0 - tighten_margin_ratio)
637: 		suggested_limit = clampf(suggested_limit, default_limit, effective_limit)
638: 		if suggested_limit >= effective_limit:
639: 			continue
640: 
641: 		suggestions.append({
642: 			"snapshot": snapshot_id,
643: 			"id": rule_id,
644: 			"reason": str(row.get("reason", "")),
645: 			"streak": current_streak,
646: 			"actual": float(row.get("actual", 0.0)),
647: 			"default_limit": default_limit,
648: 			"current_limit": effective_limit,
649: 			"suggested_limit": suggested_limit,
650: 		})
651: 
652: 	whitelist_row["streaks"] = streaks
653: 	whitelist_row["convergence_suggestions"] = suggestions
654: 	whitelist_row["stale_run_threshold"] = stale_run_threshold
655: 	whitelist_row["tighten_margin_ratio"] = tighten_margin_ratio
656: 	report["whitelist"] = whitelist_row
657: 
658: 
659: func _evaluate_exception_lifecycle(
660: 	report: Dictionary,
661: 	previous_report: Dictionary,
662: 	expire_idle_runs: int,
663: 	auto_reclaim_hit_streak: int,
664: 	max_expired_entries: int,
665: 	max_reclaim_candidates: int
666: ) -> void:
667: 	var whitelist_row: Dictionary = report.get("whitelist", {})
668: 	var current_items: Array = whitelist_row.get("items", [])
669: 	var convergence_items: Array = whitelist_row.get("convergence_suggestions", [])
670: 	var previous_whitelist: Dictionary = previous_report.get("whitelist", {})
671: 	var previous_lifecycle: Dictionary = previous_whitelist.get("lifecycle", {})
672: 
673: 	var current_items_by_key: Dictionary = {}
674: 	for item in current_items:
675: 		if not (item is Dictionary):
676: 			continue
677: 		var row: Dictionary = item
678: 		var snapshot_id: String = str(row.get("snapshot", ""))
679: 		var rule_id: String = str(row.get("id", ""))
680: 		if snapshot_id.is_empty() or rule_id.is_empty():
681: 			continue
682: 		var key: String = "%s|%s" % [snapshot_id, rule_id]
683: 		current_items_by_key[key] = row
684: 
685: 	var suggestions_by_key: Dictionary = {}
686: 	for item in convergence_items:
687: 		if not (item is Dictionary):
688: 			continue
689: 		var row: Dictionary = item
690: 		var snapshot_id: String = str(row.get("snapshot", ""))
691: 		var rule_id: String = str(row.get("id", ""))
692: 		if snapshot_id.is_empty() or rule_id.is_empty():
693: 			continue
694: 		var key: String = "%s|%s" % [snapshot_id, rule_id]
695: 		suggestions_by_key[key] = row
696: 
697: 	var all_keys: Dictionary = {}
698: 	for key_var in previous_lifecycle.keys():
699: 		all_keys[str(key_var)] = true
700: 	for key_var in current_items_by_key.keys():
701: 		all_keys[str(key_var)] = true
702: 
703: 	var lifecycle: Dictionary = {}
704: 	var expired_entries: Array = []
705: 	var reclaim_candidates: Array = []
706: 
707: 	for key_var in all_keys.keys():
708: 		var key: String = str(key_var)
709: 		var previous_row_variant: Variant = previous_lifecycle.get(key, {})
710: 		var previous_row: Dictionary = previous_row_variant if previous_row_variant is Dictionary else {}
711: 		var snapshot_id: String = str(previous_row.get("snapshot", ""))
712: 		var rule_id: String = str(previous_row.get("id", ""))
713: 		var hit_streak: int = int(previous_row.get("hit_streak", 0))
714: 		var idle_runs: int = int(previous_row.get("idle_runs", 0))
715: 		var total_hits: int = int(previous_row.get("total_hits", 0))
716: 		var last_status: String = "idle"
717: 
718: 		if current_items_by_key.has(key):
719: 			var current_row_variant: Variant = current_items_by_key.get(key, {})
720: 			var current_row: Dictionary = current_row_variant if current_row_variant is Dictionary else {}
721: 			snapshot_id = str(current_row.get("snapshot", snapshot_id))
722: 			rule_id = str(current_row.get("id", rule_id))
723: 			hit_streak += 1
724: 			idle_runs = 0
725: 			total_hits += 1
726: 			last_status = "hit"
727: 		else:
728: 			hit_streak = 0
729: 			idle_runs += 1

782: 
783: func _capture_snapshot(snapshot_id: String, row: Dictionary, sample_rounds: int) -> Dictionary:
784: 	var scene_path: String = str(row.get("scene", ""))
785: 	if scene_path.is_empty() or not scene_path.begins_with("res://"):
786: 		return {"ok": false, "message": "invalid scene path"}
787: 	if not ResourceLoader.exists(scene_path):
788: 		return {"ok": false, "message": "scene does not exist"}
789: 
790: 	var packed_scene: PackedScene = load(scene_path) as PackedScene
791: 	if packed_scene == null:
792: 		return {"ok": false, "message": "scene load failed"}
793: 
794: 	var instance: Node = packed_scene.instantiate()
795: 	if instance == null:
796: 		return {"ok": false, "message": "scene instantiate failed"}
797: 
798: 	var width: int = maxi(320, int(row.get("width", 1280)))
799: 	var height: int = maxi(180, int(row.get("height", 720)))
800: 	var warmup_frames: int = maxi(1, int(row.get("warmup_frames", 12)))
801: 	var capture_frames: int = maxi(1, int(row.get("capture_frames", 4)))
802: 	var setup: String = str(row.get("setup", "none"))
803: 
804: 	var viewport: SubViewport = SubViewport.new()
805: 	viewport.disable_3d = true
806: 	viewport.transparent_bg = false
807: 	viewport.render_target_update_mode = SubViewport.UPDATE_ALWAYS
808: 	viewport.render_target_clear_mode = SubViewport.CLEAR_MODE_ALWAYS
809: 	viewport.size = Vector2i(width, height)
810: 	get_root().add_child(viewport)
811: 	viewport.add_child(instance)
812: 
813: 	await _wait_frames(warmup_frames)
814: 	_apply_snapshot_setup(instance, setup)
815: 	await _wait_frames(capture_frames)
816: 	var output_path: String = "%s/%s.png" % [SNAPSHOT_DIR, _sanitize_snapshot_id(snapshot_id)]
817: 	var rounds: Array[Dictionary] = []
818: 	var rounds_count: int = maxi(1, sample_rounds)
819: 	for round_idx in range(rounds_count):
820: 		RenderingServer.force_draw()
821: 		await _wait_frames(1)
822: 		rounds.append(_capture_single_metrics(viewport, instance, width, height, output_path))
823: 
824: 	var metrics: Dictionary = _aggregate_round_metrics(rounds)
825: 	viewport.queue_free()
826: 	await _wait_frames(1)
827: 	return {
828: 		"ok": true,
829: 		"metrics": metrics,
830: 	}
831: 
832: 
833: func _capture_single_metrics(viewport: SubViewport, instance: Node, width: int, height: int, output_path: String) -> Dictionary:
834: 	if DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless":
835: 		var fallback_metrics_headless: Dictionary = _compute_fallback_scene_metrics(instance, width, height)
836: 		fallback_metrics_headless["image_path"] = ""
837: 		fallback_metrics_headless["capture_mode"] = "fallback_headless_display"
838: 		return fallback_metrics_headless
839: 
840: 	var texture: Texture2D = viewport.get_texture()
841: 	if texture == null:
842: 		var fallback_metrics: Dictionary = _compute_fallback_scene_metrics(instance, width, height)
843: 		fallback_metrics["image_path"] = ""
844: 		fallback_metrics["capture_mode"] = "fallback_headless"
845: 		return fallback_metrics
846: 
847: 	var image: Image = texture.get_image()
848: 	if image == null or image.is_empty():
849: 		var fallback_metrics_empty: Dictionary = _compute_fallback_scene_metrics(instance, width, height)
850: 		fallback_metrics_empty["image_path"] = ""
851: 		fallback_metrics_empty["capture_mode"] = "fallback_empty_image"
852: 		return fallback_metrics_empty
853: 
854: 	image.convert(Image.FORMAT_RGBA8)
855: 	var metrics: Dictionary = _compute_image_metrics(image)
856: 	var save_error: int = image.save_png(output_path)
857: 	if save_error != OK:
858: 		var fallback_metrics_save: Dictionary = _compute_fallback_scene_metrics(instance, width, height)
859: 		fallback_metrics_save["image_path"] = ""
860: 		fallback_metrics_save["capture_mode"] = "fallback_save_error"
861: 		return fallback_metrics_save
862: 
863: 	metrics["image_path"] = output_path
864: 	metrics["capture_mode"] = "image"
865: 	return metrics
866: 
867: 
868: func _aggregate_round_metrics(rounds: Array[Dictionary]) -> Dictionary:
869: 	var round_count: int = rounds.size()
870: 	if round_count <= 0:
871: 		return {}
872: 
873: 	var opaque_values: Array[float] = []
874: 	var luma_values: Array[float] = []
875: 	var color_values: Array[float] = []
876: 	var modes: Dictionary = {}
877: 	var sampled_pixels_sum: float = 0.0
878: 	var width: int = int(rounds[0].get("width", 0))
879: 	var height: int = int(rounds[0].get("height", 0))
880: 	var image_path: String = ""
881: 
882: 	for row in rounds:
883: 		var opaque_ratio: float = float(row.get("opaque_ratio", 0.0))
884: 		var avg_luma: float = float(row.get("avg_luma", 0.0))
885: 		var unique_colors: float = float(row.get("unique_colors", 0))
886: 		var sampled_pixels: float = float(row.get("sampled_pixels", 0))
887: 		opaque_values.append(opaque_ratio)
888: 		luma_values.append(avg_luma)
889: 		color_values.append(unique_colors)
890: 		sampled_pixels_sum += sampled_pixels
891: 
892: 		var mode: String = str(row.get("capture_mode", "unknown"))
893: 		modes[mode] = int(modes.get(mode, 0)) + 1
894: 
895: 		if image_path.is_empty():
896: 			var image_candidate: String = str(row.get("image_path", ""))
897: 			if not image_candidate.is_empty():
898: 				image_path = image_candidate
899: 
900: 	var opaque_avg: float = _avg(opaque_values)
901: 	var luma_avg: float = _avg(luma_values)
902: 	var unique_avg: float = _avg(color_values)
903: 	var opaque_stddev: float = _stddev(opaque_values, opaque_avg)
904: 	var luma_stddev: float = _stddev(luma_values, luma_avg)
905: 	var unique_stddev: float = _stddev(color_values, unique_avg)
906: 	var unique_stddev_ratio: float = unique_stddev / maxf(1.0, unique_avg)
907: 	var sampled_pixels_avg: int = int(round(sampled_pixels_sum / float(round_count)))
908: 	var opaque_pixels_avg: int = int(round(float(sampled_pixels_avg) * opaque_avg))
909: 
910: 	return {
911: 		"width": width,
912: 		"height": height,
913: 		"sample_rounds": round_count,
914: 		"sampled_pixels": sampled_pixels_avg,
915: 		"opaque_pixels": opaque_pixels_avg,
916: 		"opaque_ratio": opaque_avg,
917: 		"opaque_ratio_stddev": opaque_stddev,
918: 		"unique_colors": int(round(unique_avg)),
919: 		"unique_colors_stddev": unique_stddev,
920: 		"unique_color_stddev_ratio": unique_stddev_ratio,
921: 		"avg_luma": luma_avg,
922: 		"avg_luma_stddev": luma_stddev,
923: 		"capture_mode": _pick_dominant_mode(modes),
924: 		"capture_modes": modes,
925: 		"image_path": image_path,
926: 	}
927: 
928: 
929: func _avg(values: Array[float]) -> float:
930: 	if values.is_empty():
931: 		return 0.0
932: 	var total: float = 0.0
933: 	for v in values:
934: 		total += v
935: 	return total / float(values.size())
936: 
937: 
938: func _stddev(values: Array[float], mean: float) -> float:
939: 	if values.size() <= 1:
940: 		return 0.0
941: 	var sum_sq: float = 0.0
942: 	for v in values:
943: 		var delta: float = v - mean
944: 		sum_sq += delta * delta
945: 	return sqrt(sum_sq / float(values.size()))
946: 
947: 
948: func _pick_dominant_mode(modes: Dictionary) -> String:
949: 	var best_mode: String = "unknown"
950: 	var best_count: int = -1
951: 	for mode_var in modes.keys():
952: 		var mode: String = str(mode_var)
953: 		var count: int = int(modes.get(mode, 0))
954: 		if count > best_count:
955: 			best_mode = mode
956: 			best_count = count
957: 	return best_mode
958: 
959: 
960: func _detect_backend_tag() -> String:
961: 	var env_tag: String = OS.get_environment("VISUAL_SNAPSHOT_BACKEND_TAG").strip_edges()
962: 	if not env_tag.is_empty():
963: 		return env_tag
964: 	var os_name: String = OS.get_name().to_lower()
965: 	var display_name: String = DisplayServer.get_name().to_lower()
966: 	if display_name == "headless":
967: 		if os_name.contains("windows"):
968: 			return "windows_headless"
969: 		if os_name.contains("linux"):
970: 			return "linux_headless"
971: 		return "default"
972: 	return "default"
973:

981: 		var default_row: Variant = backend_profiles.get("default")
982: 		if default_row is Dictionary:
983: 			return default_row
984: 	return {}

1024: 		return resolved
1025: 
1026: 	var strategy_row_variant: Variant = strategies.get(selected_strategy, {})
1027: 	if not (strategy_row_variant is Dictionary):
1028: 		var errors_invalid_strategy: Array = resolved.get("errors", [])
1029: 		errors_invalid_strategy.append("strategy '%s' row must be object" % selected_strategy)
1030: 		resolved["errors"] = errors_invalid_strategy
1031: 		return resolved
1032: 	var strategy_row: Dictionary = strategy_row_variant
1033: 	var template_name: String = str(strategy_row.get("template", "")).strip_edges()
1034: 	if template_name.is_empty():
1035: 		var errors_missing_template: Array = resolved.get("errors", [])
1036: 		errors_missing_template.append("strategy '%s' missing template" % selected_strategy)
1037: 		resolved["errors"] = errors_missing_template
1038: 		return resolved
1039: 	resolved["template"] = template_name
1040: 
1041: 	var templates_variant: Variant = strategy_orchestration.get("templates", {})
1042: 	if not (templates_variant is Dictionary):
1043: 		var errors_missing_templates: Array = resolved.get("errors", [])
1044: 		errors_missing_templates.append("strategy_orchestration.templates must be object")
1045: 		resolved["errors"] = errors_missing_templates
1046: 		return resolved
1047: 	var templates: Dictionary = templates_variant

1053: 
1054: 	var template_row_variant: Variant = templates.get(template_name, {})

1060: 	var template_row: Dictionary = template_row_variant

1063: 	resolved["whitelist_policy"] = _merge_shallow_dictionary(resolved.get("whitelist_policy", {}), template_row.get("whitelist_policy", {}))

1072: 
1073: func _merge_shallow_dictionary(base_row_variant: Variant, override_row_variant: Variant) -> Dictionary:
1074: 	var output: Dictionary = {}
1075: 	if base_row_variant is Dictionary:
1076: 		var base_row: Dictionary = base_row_variant
1077: 		for key_var in base_row.keys():
1078: 			output[key_var] = base_row.get(key_var)
1079: 	if override_row_variant is Dictionary:
1080: 		var override_row: Dictionary = override_row_variant
1081: 		for key_var in override_row.keys():
1082: 			output[key_var] = override_row.get(key_var)
1083: 	return output
1084: 
1085: 
1086: func _merge_report_layer_overrides(base_layers_variant: Variant, override_layers_variant: Variant) -> Dictionary:
1087: 	var output: Dictionary = {}
1088: 	if base_layers_variant is Dictionary:
1089: 		var base_layers: Dictionary = base_layers_variant
1090: 		for layer_var in base_layers.keys():
1091: 			var layer_name: String = str(layer_var)
1092: 			var row_variant: Variant = base_layers.get(layer_var, {})
1093: 			if row_variant is Dictionary:
1094: 				output[layer_name] = _merge_shallow_dictionary(row_variant, {})
1095: 
1096: 	if not (override_layers_variant is Dictionary):
1097: 		return output
1098: 
1099: 	var override_layers: Dictionary = override_layers_variant
1100: 	for layer_var in override_layers.keys():
1101: 		var layer_name: String = str(layer_var).strip_edges()
1102: 		if layer_name.is_empty() or not output.has(layer_name):
1103: 			continue
1104: 		var override_row_variant: Variant = override_layers.get(layer_var, {})
1105: 		if not (override_row_variant is Dictionary):
1106: 			continue
1107: 		var base_row_variant: Variant = output.get(layer_name, {})
1108: 		output[layer_name] = _merge_shallow_dictionary(base_row_variant, override_row_variant)
1109: 
1110: 	return output
1111: 
1112: 
1113: func _sanitize_string_array(raw: Variant) -> Array[String]:
1114: 	var output: Array[String] = []
1115: 	if raw is Array:
1116: 		for item in raw:
1117: 			var value: String = str(item).strip_edges()
1118: 			if not value.is_empty():
1119: 				output.append(value)
1120: 	return output
1121: 
1122: 
1123: func _sanitize_allowed_capture_modes(raw: Variant) -> Dictionary:
1124: 	var output: Dictionary = {}
1125: 	if raw is Dictionary:
1126: 		for key_var in raw.keys():
1127: 			var backend: String = str(key_var).strip_edges()
1128: 			if backend.is_empty():
1129: 				continue
1130: 			output[backend] = _sanitize_string_array(raw.get(key_var, []))
1131: 	return output
1132: 
1133: 
1134: func _resolve_allowed_capture_modes(modes_map: Dictionary, backend_tag: String) -> Array[String]:
1135: 	if modes_map.has(backend_tag):
1136: 		var row: Variant = modes_map.get(backend_tag, [])
1137: 		if row is Array:
1138: 			return _sanitize_string_array(row)
1139: 	if modes_map.has("default"):
1140: 		var default_row: Variant = modes_map.get("default", [])
1141: 		if default_row is Array:
1142: 			return _sanitize_string_array(default_row)
1143: 	return []
1144: 
1145: 
1146: func _sanitize_diff_whitelist(raw: Variant) -> Dictionary:
1147: 	var output: Dictionary = {}
1148: 	if not (raw is Dictionary):
1149: 		return output
1150: 
1151: 	for backend_var in raw.keys():
1152: 		var backend: String = str(backend_var).strip_edges()
1153: 		if backend.is_empty():
1154: 			continue
1155: 		var per_backend: Variant = raw.get(backend_var, {})
1156: 		if not (per_backend is Dictionary):
1157: 			continue
1158: 		var sanitized_backend: Dictionary = {}
1159: 		for snapshot_var in per_backend.keys():
1160: 			var snapshot_id: String = str(snapshot_var).strip_edges()
1161: 			if snapshot_id.is_empty():
1162: 				continue
1163: 			var row: Variant = per_backend.get(snapshot_var, {})
1164: 			if not (row is Dictionary):
1165: 				continue
1166: 			var sanitized: Dictionary = {}
1167: 			if row.has("max_opaque_ratio_drop"):
1168: 				sanitized["max_opaque_ratio_drop"] = maxf(0.0, float(row.get("max_opaque_ratio_drop", 0.0)))
1169: 			if row.has("max_unique_color_drop_ratio"):
1170: 				sanitized["max_unique_color_drop_ratio"] = maxf(0.0, float(row.get("max_unique_color_drop_ratio", 0.0)))
1171: 			if row.has("max_luma_delta"):
1172: 				sanitized["max_luma_delta"] = maxf(0.0, float(row.get("max_luma_delta", 0.0)))
1173: 			sanitized["reason"] = str(row.get("reason", "")).strip_edges()
1174: 			sanitized_backend[snapshot_id] = sanitized
1175: 		output[backend] = sanitized_backend
1176: 
1177: 	return output
1178:

1180: func _resolve_snapshot_whitelist(whitelist: Dictionary, backend_tag: String, snapshot_id: String) -> Dictionary:
1181: 	if whitelist.has(backend_tag):

1183: 		if backend_row is Dictionary and backend_row.has(snapshot_id):
1184: 			var snapshot_row: Variant = backend_row.get(snapshot_id, {})
1185: 			if snapshot_row is Dictionary:
1186: 				return snapshot_row
1187: 	if whitelist.has("default"):
1188: 		var default_row: Variant = whitelist.get("default", {})
1189: 		if default_row is Dictionary and default_row.has(snapshot_id):
1190: 			var snapshot_default: Variant = default_row.get(snapshot_id, {})
1191: 			if snapshot_default is Dictionary:
1192: 				return snapshot_default
1193: 	return {}

1202: 	return output

1295: func _sanitize_approval_threshold_templates(
1296: 	raw: Variant,
1297: 	release_gate_templates: Dictionary,
1298: 	backend_matrix_governance: Dictionary
1299: ) -> Dictionary:
1300: 	var output: Dictionary = {
1301: 		"default_template": "standard",
1302: 		"run_mode_templates": {},
1303: 		"templates": {},
1304: 	}
1305: 	if not (raw is Dictionary):
1306: 		return output
1307: 
1308: 	var row: Dictionary = raw
1309: 	var default_template: String = str(row.get("default_template", "standard")).strip_edges()
1310: 	if default_template.is_empty():
1311: 		default_template = "standard"
1312: 	output["default_template"] = default_template
1313: 
1314: 	var run_mode_templates: Dictionary = {}
1315: 	var run_mode_templates_raw: Variant = row.get("run_mode_templates", {})
1316: 	if run_mode_templates_raw is Dictionary:
1317: 		for run_mode_var in run_mode_templates_raw.keys():
1318: 			var mode_name: String = str(run_mode_var).strip_edges()
1319: 			var template_name: String = str(run_mode_templates_raw.get(run_mode_var, "")).strip_edges()
1320: 			if mode_name.is_empty() or template_name.is_empty():
1321: 				continue
1322: 			run_mode_templates[mode_name] = template_name
1323: 	output["run_mode_templates"] = run_mode_templates
1324: 
1325: 	var templates: Dictionary = {}
1326: 	var templates_raw: Variant = row.get("templates", {})
1327: 	if templates_raw is Dictionary:
1328: 		for template_var in templates_raw.keys():
1329: 			var template_name: String = str(template_var).strip_edges()
1330: 			if template_name.is_empty():
1331: 				continue
1332: 			var template_row_variant: Variant = templates_raw.get(template_var, {})
1333: 			if not (template_row_variant is Dictionary):
1334: 				continue
1335: 			var template_row: Dictionary = template_row_variant
1336: 			templates[template_name] = {
1337: 				"approval_workflow": _merge_shallow_dictionary({}, template_row.get("approval_workflow", {})),
1338: 				"approval_audit_trail": _merge_shallow_dictionary({}, template_row.get("approval_audit_trail", {})),
1339: 				"approval_history_archive": _merge_shallow_dictionary({}, template_row.get("approval_history_archive", {})),
1340: 			}
1341: 	output["templates"] = templates
1342: 
1343: 	if not run_mode_templates.has("release_candidate") and (release_gate_templates.get("ci_mode_bindings", {}) as Dictionary).has("release_candidate"):
1344: 		run_mode_templates["release_candidate"] = "candidate"
1345: 	if not run_mode_templates.has("release_blocking") and (release_gate_templates.get("ci_mode_bindings", {}) as Dictionary).has("release_blocking"):
1346: 		run_mode_templates["release_blocking"] = "blocking"
1347: 
1348: 	var required_run_modes: Array[String] = _sanitize_string_array(backend_matrix_governance.get("required_run_modes", []))
1349: 	for mode_name in required_run_modes:
1350: 		if not run_mode_templates.has(mode_name):
1351: 			run_mode_templates[mode_name] = default_template
1352: 	output["run_mode_templates"] = run_mode_templates
1353: 
1354: 	return output
1355: 
1356: 
1357: func _resolve_approval_threshold_template(raw: Dictionary, run_mode: String, base_sections: Dictionary) -> Dictionary:
1358: 	var resolved: Dictionary = {
1359: 		"template": "standard",
1360: 		"errors": [],
1361: 		"approval_workflow": base_sections.get("approval_workflow", {}),
1362: 		"approval_audit_trail": base_sections.get("approval_audit_trail", {}),
1363: 		"approval_history_archive": base_sections.get("approval_history_archive", {}),
1364: 	}
1365: 	if raw.is_empty():
1366: 		return resolved
1367: 
1368: 	var default_template: String = str(raw.get("default_template", "standard")).strip_edges()
1369: 	if default_template.is_empty():
1370: 		default_template = "standard"
1371: 	var run_mode_templates: Dictionary = raw.get("run_mode_templates", {})
1372: 	var templates: Dictionary = raw.get("templates", {})
1373: 
1374: 	var selected_template: String = OS.get_environment("VISUAL_APPROVAL_TEMPLATE").strip_edges()
1375: 	if selected_template.is_empty() and run_mode_templates.has(run_mode):
1376: 		selected_template = str(run_mode_templates.get(run_mode, "")).strip_edges()
1377: 	if selected_template.is_empty():
1378: 		selected_template = default_template
1379: 	if selected_template.is_empty():
1380: 		selected_template = "standard"
1381: 	resolved["template"] = selected_template
1382: 
1383: 	if not templates.has(selected_template):
1384: 		var errors_missing_template: Array = resolved.get("errors", [])
1385: 		errors_missing_template.append("approval_threshold_templates template '%s' not found" % selected_template)
1386: 		resolved["errors"] = errors_missing_template
1387: 		return resolved
1388: 
1389: 	var template_row_variant: Variant = templates.get(selected_template, {})
1390: 	if not (template_row_variant is Dictionary):
1391: 		var errors_invalid_template: Array = resolved.get("errors", [])
1392: 		errors_invalid_template.append("approval_threshold_templates template '%s' must be object" % selected_template)
1393: 		resolved["errors"] = errors_invalid_template
1394: 		return resolved
1395: 
1396: 	var template_row: Dictionary = template_row_variant
1397: 	resolved["approval_workflow"] = _merge_shallow_dictionary(
1398: 		resolved.get("approval_workflow", {}),
1399: 		template_row.get("approval_workflow", {})
1400: 	)
1401: 	resolved["approval_audit_trail"] = _merge_shallow_dictionary(
1402: 		resolved.get("approval_audit_trail", {}),
1403: 		template_row.get("approval_audit_trail", {})
1404: 	)
1405: 	resolved["approval_history_archive"] = _merge_shallow_dictionary(
1406: 		resolved.get("approval_history_archive", {}),
1407: 		template_row.get("approval_history_archive", {})
1408: 	)
1409: 
1410: 	return resolved
1411:

1413: func _sanitize_release_candidate_tracking(raw: Variant) -> Dictionary:

1416: 		"window": 40,
1417: 		"required_run_modes": ["release_candidate"],
1418: 		"required_strategies": ["release_candidate"],
1419: 		"min_runs": 1,
1420: 		"max_avg_warnings": 1.0,
1421: 		"max_total_blockers": 0,
1422: 		"max_tracking_failures": 0,
1423: 	}
1424: 	if not (raw is Dictionary):
1425: 		return output
1426: 	var row: Dictionary = raw

1428: 	if history_file.begins_with("user://"):
1429: 		output["history_file"] = history_file
1430: 	output["window"] = maxi(5, int(row.get("window", output.get("window", 40))))
1431: 	var required_run_modes: Array[String] = _sanitize_string_array(row.get("required_run_modes", []))
1432: 	if not required_run_modes.is_empty():
1433: 		output["required_run_modes"] = required_run_modes
1434: 	var required_strategies: Array[String] = _sanitize_string_array(row.get("required_strategies", []))
1435: 	if not required_strategies.is_empty():
1436: 		output["required_strategies"] = required_strategies
1437: 	output["min_runs"] = maxi(1, int(row.get("min_runs", output.get("min_runs", 1))))
1438: 	output["max_avg_warnings"] = maxf(0.0, float(row.get("max_avg_warnings", output.get("max_avg_warnings", 1.0))))
1439: 	output["max_total_blockers"] = maxi(0, int(row.get("max_total_blockers", output.get("max_total_blockers", 0))))
1440: 	output["max_tracking_failures"] = maxi(0, int(row.get("max_tracking_failures", output.get("max_tracking_failures", 0))))
1441: 	return output

1500: func _sanitize_report_layers(raw: Variant, snapshots_raw: Variant) -> Dictionary:

1516: 				continue
1517: 			if not snapshots.has(snapshot_id):
1518: 				continue
1519: 			snapshot_ids.append(snapshot_id)
